review_method: Model-assisted structural review of every supplied evidence note; no systematic full-text verification of the cited corpus

framing_assumptions: A staffing concern at a high-containment facility is treated as relevant to the probability of a specific accident involving a possibly different laboratory workflow.
policy_sensitivities: Weight changes sharply depending on whether the policy admits retrospective circumstantial indicators or requires incident-specific evidence.
coverage_gaps: The complete cable context, facility-specific incident records, and the containment level of the hypothesized precursor work are not established by this item.
correlation_risks: Overlaps with the BSL-2 handling and general WIV biosafety items; these should not be counted as three independent accident signals.

evidential: The cable documents a concern but does not document an accident.
ingestion_conditional: Interpretation depends on whether the full cable or selected quotations and later commentary are used.
framing_conditional: The same document can be framed as a warning, a request for cooperation, or a routine capacity assessment.

notes: Primarily bears on opportunity and institutional risk, not direct causation.

framing_assumptions: The observed early-case set is sufficiently representative for absence of a second cluster to be informative.
policy_sensitivities: Assessment changes with case-definition rules, treatment of market environmental samples, and how ascertainment-bias critiques are weighted.
coverage_gaps: Independent raw line-list data and a complete account of early case-finding procedures are unavailable.
correlation_risks: Substantially overlaps the market-spatial-clustering and two-lineage evidence cluster.

evidential: Competing statistical explanations remain for the observed spatial pattern.
ingestion_conditional: The corpus depends on Chinese-curated early-case and environmental records.
framing_conditional: A market epicenter is not automatically identical to the site of the first cross-species or laboratory-associated infection.

notes: Separate descriptive facts about observed cases from the inference that no relevant off-market cluster existed.

framing_assumptions: Natural availability of ACE2-binding relatives materially addresses origin hypotheses centered on an allegedly unnatural receptor-binding domain.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on whether the target hypothesis is rational RBD design, any engineering, serial passage, or research-related collection of a natural virus.
coverage_gaps: Known BANAL viruses do not bridge the complete genomic gap or explain every SARS-CoV-2 feature, including the furin-cleavage-site question.
correlation_risks: Shares mechanism-level support with natural-recombination, pangolin-RBD, and genetic-distance items.

evidential: The viruses demonstrate natural ACE2 binding but have important phenotypic and genomic differences from SARS-CoV-2.
ingestion_conditional: Inference is limited by sparse sampling of the wider sarbecovirus reservoir.
framing_conditional: Strong evidence against one rational-design argument is weaker evidence against the broader lab-incident hypothesis.

notes: Avoid upgrading a rebuttal to a narrow engineering claim into proof of the complete zoonotic pathway.

framing_assumptions: A rare codon pair is assumed to be more expected under deliberate construction than under natural evolution.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on the reference class used for codon rarity and on whether designer preferences are specified before observing the sequence.
coverage_gaps: A preregistered likelihood model for the doublet under competing mechanisms is absent.
correlation_risks: This is one feature of the broader furin-cleavage-site cluster and should not be counted independently from it.

evidential: The observation is real but its likelihood ratio is poorly established.
framing_conditional: Calling the doublet a fingerprint embeds an unstated model of how an engineer would design the sequence.

notes: Treat as a low-directness feature argument rather than an independent origin event.

framing_assumptions: Institutional opacity is treated as evidence about the concealed event rather than a general feature of the political environment.
policy_sensitivities: Weight changes with the assumed base rate of data restriction in unrelated Chinese crises and with whether process evidence is admitted into causal assessment.
coverage_gaps: The contents of still-unreleased datasets and the reasons for each restriction are, by definition, unavailable.
correlation_risks: Highly overlaps database-offline, data-deletion, WHO-access, and RaTG13-disclosure items.

evidential: Opacity lowers confidence in all conclusions but is weakly directional between origin hypotheses.
ingestion_conditional: Missing data directly limits what the pipeline can assess.
framing_conditional: The same conduct can be framed as concealment of a lab event, concealment of regulatory failure, or generic authoritarian control.

notes: Best represented as a coverage and trust modifier, not a standalone vote for either origin.

framing_assumptions: Several heterogeneous access events are grouped as one pattern of suspicious conduct.
policy_sensitivities: The result depends on whether the policy aggregates events by apparent motive or evaluates each event's ordinary administrative base rate separately.
coverage_gaps: Server logs, submitter correspondence, retained database contents, and complete access histories are missing.
correlation_risks: Contains the same underlying events represented separately by database-offline and China-opacity items.

evidential: Deletion or restriction is established more readily than a concealment motive or its relevance to origin.
ingestion_conditional: Unavailable content prevents assessing whether the missing records would be directionally informative.
framing_conditional: Bundling events creates a stronger pattern than evaluating their distinct causes may warrant.

notes: Use event-level nodes and shared-event identifiers to avoid pattern inflation.

framing_assumptions: A proposed experimental capability and conceptual match are treated as evidence that related work may have been performed.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on required match specificity: general cleavage-site work, S1/S2 furin insertion, or the exact SARS-CoV-2 sequence and backbone.
coverage_gaps: No verified execution record, precursor sequence, laboratory notebook, or chain connecting DEFUSE work to SARS-CoV-2 is supplied.
correlation_risks: One of three retained DEFUSE write-ups describing the same proposal; the cluster must count once.

evidential: The proposal is documented; execution and causal connection are not.
ingestion_conditional: Assessment is sensitive to access to unpublished work records and complete proposal correspondence.
framing_conditional: A blueprint can be treated as opportunity evidence or, more strongly and controversially, as a near-match to the event.

notes: Preserve the distinction between capability, intention, execution, and causation.

framing_assumptions: The coincidence between a proposed intervention and a later viral feature is treated as unlikely without a causal connection.
policy_sensitivities: Weight changes with priors for natural cleavage-site emergence and for unfunded or pilot work proceeding outside the grant.
coverage_gaps: Evidence of execution, the exact construct design, and a matching precursor are absent.
correlation_risks: Duplicate DEFUSE evidence cluster; strongly correlated with the furin-site and laboratory-capability items.

evidential: The conceptual resemblance is real but molecular and procedural linkage is unresolved.
ingestion_conditional: Unreleased experimental records could materially change the assessment in either direction.
framing_conditional: Calling the proposal a blueprint gives more causal force than calling it evidence of relevant research interest.

notes: Do not add this item's weight to the other DEFUSE summaries.

framing_assumptions: Documented intent and capability among named actors are treated as increasing the probability of undocumented execution.
policy_sensitivities: Assessment depends on whether primary proposal text, later advocacy interpretations, or molecular-specificity criteria dominate source selection.
coverage_gaps: No direct record shows the proposed experiment occurred or produced a SARS-CoV-2-like virus.
correlation_risks: Third representation of the same DEFUSE proposal; duplicate prose broadens citations but not evidential independence.

evidential: Proposal existence is high-confidence; causal implication is low-directness.
ingestion_conditional: The inference is bounded by absent laboratory and funding records.
framing_conditional: The label blueprint can obscure the gap between a general experimental plan and the specific observed virus.

notes: Model this as the same underlying event as the two other DEFUSE documents.

framing_assumptions: Lack of reported market exposure is treated as evidence that the market was not the origin rather than as expected incomplete linkage during community spread.
policy_sensitivities: Conclusion changes with case inclusion dates, direct-versus-geographic linkage rules, and confidence in official case summaries.
coverage_gaps: Raw patient line lists, complete exposure histories, and independent early surveillance records are unavailable.
correlation_risks: Uses the same early-case dataset as market-clustering and absence-of-non-market-cluster items, sometimes to argue the opposite direction.

evidential: Reported non-linkage is descriptive; its causal implication is contested.
ingestion_conditional: Case histories were summarized and curated rather than independently available.
framing_conditional: Direct exposure, residence near the market, and outbreak epicenter are distinct relationships.

notes: Represent opposing interpretations as reviews of one shared dataset, not independent evidence nodes.

framing_assumptions: Spatial concentration of viral material at wildlife stalls is treated as evidence of infected animals rather than human shedding in a transmission site.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on whether environmental co-location without a positive animal is admitted as direct, indirect, or merely contextual evidence.
coverage_gaps: No pre-closure animal samples, complete stall-level sampling frame, or direction-of-shedding evidence is available.
correlation_risks: Overlaps raccoon-dog co-location, live-mammal sales, and general market-spatial evidence.

evidential: Co-location is established more strongly than the animal-to-human direction of transmission.
ingestion_conditional: Sampling occurred after market closure and after human transmission, limiting causal resolution.
framing_conditional: An amplifier-market and a spillover-market can produce similar environmental patterns.

notes: Keep observation, mechanism, and origin-site inference as separate claims.

framing_assumptions: Agency expertise and classified access are treated as informative even when the underlying evidence cannot be inspected.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on deference policy, confidence-language interpretation, and whether shared intelligence inputs are treated as independent judgments.
coverage_gaps: Underlying intelligence, agency likelihood models, dissent, and reasons for assessment changes are largely classified.
correlation_risks: Agencies share information; this overlaps the ODNI split and House-report items and is not three independent evidence streams.

evidential: Public confidence levels are low to moderate and do not expose the evidence basis.
ingestion_conditional: The public corpus excludes classified inputs available to the agencies.
capability_conditional: An external reviewer cannot audit classified-source credibility or analytic tradecraft.
framing_conditional: Agency conclusions are expert assessments about evidence, not new primary evidence.

notes: Render as opaque deference evidence with an explicit correlation warning.

framing_assumptions: Uniqueness among sampled close relatives is treated as evidence of artificial introduction rather than incomplete natural sampling.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on which coronavirus reference class is used and whether feature rarity is converted into a preregistered likelihood ratio.
coverage_gaps: The natural sarbecovirus reservoir is sparsely sampled, and no complete experimental-record corpus is available.
correlation_risks: Central node shared by DEFUSE, CGG-codon, natural-recombination, and serial-passage arguments.

evidential: The feature is unusual in the sampled subgenus, but mechanism discrimination remains contested.
ingestion_conditional: Future natural sampling or laboratory records could strongly change its weight.
framing_conditional: Unusual is not synonymous with engineered; the inference requires explicit competing-mechanism priors.

notes: Treat as a contested feature requiring several dependent subarguments, not a standalone smoking gun.

framing_assumptions: A congressional committee conclusion is treated as epistemic evidence rather than a political synthesis of already-available evidence.
policy_sensitivities: Weight changes with policies for partisan institutions, expert testimony, and separation of primary evidence from institutional judgment.
coverage_gaps: The item does not identify a new public primary datum uniquely supporting the report's bottom-line conclusion.
correlation_risks: Repackages DEFUSE, biosafety, intelligence, and conduct evidence already represented elsewhere.

evidential: The committee's conclusion is clear; the incremental evidential content is uncertain.
framing_conditional: Institutional conclusions can be evidence of serious consideration without being independent evidence of the event.

notes: Separate the report's primary documents from its evaluative conclusion.

framing_assumptions: The reconstructed early-case locations are representative enough for spatial centrality to identify an outbreak epicenter.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on null model, case-set definition, ascertainment-bias treatment, and distinction between epicenter and spillover site.
coverage_gaps: Independent raw line-list data, original geocoding, and complete case-finding procedures are unavailable.
correlation_risks: Same market/case dataset underlies several natural-origin evidence items and should be clustered.

evidential: Spatial centrality is supported for the available dataset; causal interpretation remains disputed.
ingestion_conditional: The available dataset is reconstructed from official reports and figures rather than independently released raw data.
capability_conditional: Adjudicating competing spatial models requires specialist statistical review.
framing_conditional: The first detected transmission center may differ from the upstream origin event.

notes: A strong candidate for the first full-content audit because it is load-bearing and methodologically contested.

framing_assumptions: Spatial clustering and two early lineages are combined as mutually reinforcing evidence of repeated market spillover.
policy_sensitivities: Result changes with spatial ascertainment assumptions and with the phylogenetic model used to infer one versus multiple introductions.
coverage_gaps: Raw early-case data, complete early sequences, and direct animal infections are absent.
correlation_risks: Composite of two separately repeated evidence clusters; combining them can hide dependence and double counting.

evidential: Both spatial and lineage patterns are observed, but each causal inference is independently contested.
ingestion_conditional: Both components depend on incomplete early Chinese data.
framing_conditional: Convergence is weaker when the components share the same market-linked sampling process.

notes: Decompose this compound item before assigning weight.

framing_assumptions: Presence of susceptible animals creates a mechanism-bearing prior for market spillover even without an identified infected animal.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on whether opportunity evidence is evaluated symmetrically for wildlife-trade and laboratory scenarios.
coverage_gaps: Animals present during the relevant period were removed before virological sampling; supply-chain infection data are incomplete.
correlation_risks: Overlaps environmental-stall and raccoon-dog genetic evidence; all bear on the same market-animal mechanism.

evidential: Animal presence and susceptibility are better established than infection and transmission.
ingestion_conditional: Missing contemporaneous animal samples prevent direct confirmation.
framing_conditional: Opportunity supports plausibility but is weaker than evidence of the actual causal event.

notes: Compare explicitly with laboratory-opportunity evidence using the same evidential category.

framing_assumptions: Demonstrated capability to construct a SARS-like chimera is treated as evidence relevant to a later event involving different actors, viruses, and records.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on whether general capability evidence is admitted and how collaboration links are translated into event probability.
coverage_gaps: No specific construct, precursor, experiment, or chain connects SHC014 work to SARS-CoV-2.
correlation_risks: Correlated with DEFUSE, WIV biosafety, and general gain-of-function capability arguments.

evidential: Capability is documented; relevance to the specific origin event is indirect.
framing_conditional: A method's existence establishes possibility, not that it was used in the event under investigation.

notes: Keep method capability separate from actor opportunity and event evidence.

framing_assumptions: Ambiguity around miner illness and sample naming is treated as relevant to whether WIV possessed a closer precursor.
policy_sensitivities: Weight changes with policies for contested theses, later addenda, advocacy reconstructions, and institutional disclosure conduct.
coverage_gaps: Complete miner clinical samples, WIV sample inventory, sequencing history, and database records are unavailable.
correlation_risks: Shares underlying mine, RaTG13, and disclosure events with the RaTG13-secrecy item.

evidential: Mine sampling and RaTG13 provenance are documented; the miner-passage narrative is not genetically established.
ingestion_conditional: Missing sample records and untranslated or inaccessible materials constrain assessment.
framing_conditional: Provenance irregularity can be treated as ordinary scientific disclosure failure or evidence of concealment.

notes: Neutral/context classification is appropriate unless a direct precursor link is established.

framing_assumptions: The estimated common-ancestor date constrains competing emergence narratives and the duration of unobserved circulation.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on sequence inclusion, clock model, recombination assumptions, and treatment of uncertain early genomes.
coverage_gaps: The earliest infections and potentially informative unsampled or withheld sequences are absent.
correlation_risks: Correlated with early-case timing and lineage-introduction analyses that use overlapping sequence data.

evidential: Clock estimates have model and sampling uncertainty even when studies agree broadly on timing.
ingestion_conditional: Missing early sequences can shift estimated ancestry and interval width.
capability_conditional: Independent assessment requires sequence-level phylogenetic analysis.

notes: Use as a temporal constraint, not a direct discriminator unless a hypothesis makes a distinct timing prediction.

framing_assumptions: Demonstrating a natural mechanism for mosaic genomes reduces the need to posit engineering for SARS-CoV-2's feature combination.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on whether mechanism plausibility or identification of the actual ancestral pathway is required.
coverage_gaps: No sampled recombination chain reconstructs the complete SARS-CoV-2 progenitor.
correlation_risks: Mechanistically overlaps BANAL, pangolin-RBD, genetic-distance, and furin-site natural-origin arguments.

evidential: Natural recombination is well established generally; its specific historical role here remains inferred.
ingestion_conditional: Sparse wildlife sampling limits reconstruction of the actual pathway.
framing_conditional: A natural mechanism rebuts necessity of engineering but does not exclude collection or laboratory passage of a natural virus.

notes: Distinguish mechanism sufficiency from event identification.

framing_assumptions: Failure to find a close progenitor or host is treated as less expected under zoonosis than under a laboratory incident.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on search-effort estimates, reservoir size, political access, and expected discovery time under each hypothesis.
coverage_gaps: The relevant wildlife reservoir, farms, supply chains, and laboratory inventories were not comprehensively sampled.
correlation_risks: Overlaps no-source-animal and RaTG13/BANAL genetic-distance items.

evidential: The absence is real in the observed corpus, but the likelihood of non-discovery under each hypothesis is uncertain.
ingestion_conditional: Sampling and access limitations directly determine the observed absence.
framing_conditional: Absence of an animal link and absence of a laboratory precursor are symmetric missing-link problems unless search efforts are compared.

notes: Requires an explicit missingness model before receiving directional weight.

framing_assumptions: A zoonotic market origin should have produced a detectable infected animal despite the delay and removal of live animals.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on the counterfactual probability of detection given actual sampling timing and supply-chain disruption.
coverage_gaps: No contemporaneous animal testing occurred at the market, and upstream farms and suppliers were incompletely investigated.
correlation_risks: Narrower version of the no-close-progenitor/intermediate-host evidence and correlated with market-animal items.

evidential: No positive source animal has been reported; the expected detection rate is unresolved.
ingestion_conditional: Late and incomplete sampling makes the negative result coverage-dependent.
framing_conditional: Not finding an animal is not equivalent to sampling the relevant animals and obtaining negative results.

notes: Represent as a failed-detection claim with explicit search coverage.

framing_assumptions: A split among intelligence agencies is treated as evidence that both hypotheses remain plausible and that public evidence is incomplete.
policy_sensitivities: Interpretation depends on deference to classified expertise and whether agency counts or confidence-weighted reasoning are used.
coverage_gaps: Underlying reports, sources, interagency dependencies, and analytic methods are classified.
correlation_risks: Same institutional evidence family as the FBI/DOE/CIA and House-report items.

evidential: The split and confidence labels are public; their evidential basis is not.
ingestion_conditional: Public assessors cannot ingest the classified corpus.
capability_conditional: Agency tradecraft and source reliability cannot be independently scored from summaries.
framing_conditional: Disagreement may indicate genuine evidential ambiguity, different agency access, or different analytic priors.

notes: Useful as a transparency limit and disagreement record, not a poll of independent experts.

framing_assumptions: Natural occurrence of similar RBD residues in pangolin coronaviruses demonstrates a plausible natural source for that genomic component.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on whether mosaic feature plausibility or a complete direct progenitor is required.
coverage_gaps: Pangolin viruses are not close whole-genome progenitors, and the precise recombination pathway is unsampled.
correlation_risks: Correlated with natural-recombination and BANAL ACE2-binding evidence.

evidential: Shared residues support natural availability but do not identify the host or route of emergence.
ingestion_conditional: Wildlife-virus sampling remains sparse and geographically uneven.
framing_conditional: Evidence against an unprecedented engineered RBD is not proof of a pangolin-mediated spillover.

notes: Keep component-level ancestry distinct from whole-virus ancestry.

framing_assumptions: Private uncertainty, rapid opinion change, or institutional coordination is treated as evidence about the reliability or independence of the published conclusion.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on whether discourse/process evidence is admitted and how preliminary private deliberation is distinguished from suppression.
coverage_gaps: Complete message context, evolving analyses, interviews, and contemporaneous evidence available to participants are not fully represented.
correlation_risks: Overlaps broader conduct, opacity, and institutional-trust evidence rather than providing new biological evidence.

evidential: Private doubts are documented; coercion, bad faith, and the scientific correctness of the final paper are separate claims.
ingestion_conditional: Selective message release can materially alter perceived narrative.
framing_conditional: Normal scientific updating and politically influenced consensus formation can resemble one another in partial records.

notes: Primarily changes trust and deference weights; it is not direct origin evidence.

framing_assumptions: Co-location of susceptible-animal DNA and viral RNA is treated as evidence that animals, rather than nearby humans, shed the virus.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on standards for metagenomic co-occurrence, contamination controls, and whether indirect animal evidence is admitted.
coverage_gaps: No intact infected animal, animal serology, or temporal sample establishes direction of transmission.
correlation_risks: Same market environmental samples support the wildlife-stall positivity item; do not count separately without decomposing claims.

evidential: Species DNA and viral RNA co-occur, but causal direction is unresolved.
ingestion_conditional: Samples were environmental and collected after market closure.
framing_conditional: The result can support an animal-source model or a contaminated-stall model depending on causal assumptions.

notes: A prime example where provenance is strong but inference structure must remain explicit.

framing_assumptions: Large divergence from known relatives is used to constrain both direct natural and known-laboratory precursor narratives.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on molecular-clock assumptions and the expected unsampled diversity in wildlife and laboratory collections.
coverage_gaps: Unknown wildlife viruses and unreleased laboratory sequences prevent a complete nearest-neighbor assessment.
correlation_risks: Shares source and mechanism base with BANAL, natural-recombination, and no-close-progenitor items.

evidential: Known viruses are too divergent to be direct progenitors; the location of the missing branch is unknown.
ingestion_conditional: The result is explicitly conditional on the viruses that have been sampled or disclosed.
framing_conditional: Distance weakens simplistic versions of both hypotheses more than it discriminates between them.

notes: Appropriately treated as common constraint rather than a directional vote.

framing_assumptions: Delayed disclosure and renaming are treated as possible evidence of concealment of a more relevant laboratory lineage.
policy_sensitivities: Weight changes with norms for virology naming, publication disclosure, and institutional transparency.
coverage_gaps: Complete sample inventories, sequence history, mine records, and internal WIV communications are unavailable.
correlation_risks: Same Mojiang/RaTG13 event as the neutral provenance item and correlated with database and opacity evidence.

evidential: Disclosure irregularities are more established than a causal relationship to SARS-CoV-2.
ingestion_conditional: Missing institutional records define the central uncertainty.
framing_conditional: The same irregularity can be framed as poor disclosure practice or purposeful concealment.

notes: Do not count the conduct framing separately from the neutral Mojiang provenance record.

framing_assumptions: A rational designer would have used then-current computational predictions and selected an apparently optimal ACE2-binding solution.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on whether the competing laboratory hypothesis specifies rational design, directed evolution, serial passage, or collection of a natural virus.
coverage_gaps: The item does not test all plausible engineering or passage workflows.
correlation_risks: Correlated with BANAL natural ACE2 binding and broader RBD-naturalness arguments.

evidential: Non-optimality relative to a model is informative mainly about a narrow design strategy.
framing_conditional: The inference weakens substantially when the lab hypothesis does not require rational computational design.

notes: Label explicitly as evidence against rational design, not against every research-related origin.

framing_assumptions: SARS-CoV-1 is selected as a close reference class for setting prior odds on SARS-CoV-2 emergence.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on reference-class choice and how differences in host discovery, surveillance, geography, and laboratory context are handled.
coverage_gaps: The analogy does not supply event-specific evidence about SARS-CoV-2.
correlation_risks: Overlaps the combined SARS-1/MERS zoonotic-precedent item.

evidential: The precedent establishes a real mechanism and prior, but analogy strength is contestable.
framing_conditional: Choosing zoonotic coronavirus precedents rather than laboratory accidents changes the prior before case evidence is considered.

notes: Represent as prior/reference-class evidence, not likelihood evidence from the Wuhan event.

framing_assumptions: The two recognized deadly coronavirus emergences form an appropriate and sufficiently complete reference class.
policy_sensitivities: Prior odds vary with whether the reference class includes all zoonoses, all laboratory accidents, unresolved outbreaks, or only close coronaviruses.
coverage_gaps: A sample of two confirmed precedents cannot establish a stable base rate and may be selected partly because those cases were resolved.
correlation_risks: Contains SARS-CoV-1 precedent again and should not be added independently to the dedicated SARS-1 item.

evidential: Both precedents are natural; their predictive relevance to this event is uncertain.
framing_conditional: Reference-class construction is a lower-layer judgment that materially sets priors.

notes: This item directly illustrates the project's claim that framing enters before assessment.

framing_assumptions: A mechanistically possible, signature-free passage pathway receives probability even without positive evidence that it occurred.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on whether possibility, demonstrated precedent, or event-specific records are required for inclusion.
coverage_gaps: No precursor, passage series, experimental record, or matching model-system result is supplied.
correlation_risks: Correlated with furin-site, laboratory-capability, WIV-biosafety, and missing-record arguments.

evidential: Plausibility differs across cell types and models; event-specific support is absent.
ingestion_conditional: Unreleased laboratory records prevent a decisive check.
framing_conditional: A hypothesis can become difficult to falsify if every absent signature is treated as expected.

notes: Keep mechanistic possibility separate from evidence that the mechanism was used.

framing_assumptions: A small cluster of autumn respiratory illness among laboratory staff is treated as anomalous relative to the seasonal baseline.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on admission of anonymous intelligence reporting and on the base rate assigned to ordinary illness and hospital visits.
coverage_gaps: Medical records, contemporaneous tests, serology, precise dates, and independently attributable identities are unavailable.
correlation_risks: News reports largely trace to overlapping intelligence claims rather than independent witnesses.

evidential: Illness may have occurred, but SARS-CoV-2 etiology is unestablished.
ingestion_conditional: The decisive medical information is not publicly accessible.
framing_conditional: Without a seasonal baseline, ordinary sickness can be selected retrospectively as a suspicious cluster.

notes: Do not treat multiple media citations to one intelligence report as corroboration.

framing_assumptions: Two early lineages and the absence of accepted intermediates are modeled as multiple introductions rather than rapid divergence after one introduction.
policy_sensitivities: Conclusion depends on topology constraints, inclusion of disputed genomes, model symmetry, and error corrections.
coverage_gaps: Early sequence sampling is incomplete, and disputed intermediate genomes and model implementations require independent reproduction.
correlation_risks: Duplicate of the two-zoonotic-introductions item and overlaps market-linked sequence evidence.

evidential: Descriptive lineage differences are stronger than the inferred number of introductions.
ingestion_conditional: Missing or excluded early genomes can alter topology.
capability_conditional: Independent adjudication requires reproducing the phylogenetic simulations and code corrections.
framing_conditional: Even rejection of two introductions does not select a lab origin over a single zoonotic introduction.

notes: Count the descriptive lineage observation once and retain competing introduction models.

framing_assumptions: The two-lineage pattern is treated as more expected from repeated animal exposures than from one introduction followed by early divergence.
policy_sensitivities: Weight changes with corrected Bayes factors, hypothesis symmetry, peer-review thresholds, and treatment of later critiques.
coverage_gaps: Full independent reproduction of the code, model constraints, and disputed early genomes is not part of the baseline.
correlation_risks: Same underlying claim as the other lineage-A/B item and part of the market evidence cluster.

evidential: The two-introduction likelihood is actively disputed and weaker after correction than early headlines implied.
ingestion_conditional: Inference depends on which sequences and critiques enter the corpus.
capability_conditional: Statistical and phylogenetic code review is required to compare the competing analyses.
framing_conditional: The hypothesis comparison may exclude plausible single-zoonosis models if conditions are asymmetric.

notes: Merge with its duplicate for weighting while preserving both citation trails.

framing_assumptions: WHO's change in public language is treated as evidence about the underlying origin rather than primarily about process and evidential sufficiency.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on deference to institutional conclusions and separation of procedural criticism from substantive probability updates.
coverage_gaps: Key data requested by WHO/SAGO remain unavailable, preventing resolution of why confidence should move.
correlation_risks: Overlaps China-data-withholding and general institutional-process evidence.

evidential: The walkback establishes premature closure more clearly than it supports a lab origin.
ingestion_conditional: Institutional uncertainty is explicitly driven by missing requested evidence.
framing_conditional: Reopening a hypothesis is not the same claim as judging it more likely.

notes: Represent as a process-correction and uncertainty signal.

framing_assumptions: Permitted BSL-2/3 work with novel bat coronaviruses is treated as a materially elevated route to the specific pandemic virus.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on contemporaneous biosafety norms, virus pathogenicity known at the time, and whether opportunity evidence is admitted.
coverage_gaps: Facility-specific procedures, incident logs, staff exposure records, and evidence that WIV possessed a direct precursor are absent.
correlation_risks: Overlaps State Department cables, Menachery/Baric capability, DEFUSE, and general lab-opportunity evidence.

evidential: Containment practices are documented more strongly than any causal accident.
ingestion_conditional: Internal incident and inventory records are not public.
framing_conditional: Practice that creates generic opportunity is not event-specific evidence unless the relevant virus and exposure are shown.

notes: Evaluate laboratory and market opportunity evidence under parallel standards.

framing_assumptions: Loss of public access shortly before recognized emergence is treated as intentional concealment related to the outbreak.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on base rates for research-site outages, exact accessibility timelines, and whether later non-release is bundled with the initial outage.
coverage_gaps: Server logs, maintenance records, internal access, complete database contents, and contemporaneous reasons are unavailable.
correlation_risks: One event already included in the broader data-deletion and China-opacity clusters.

evidential: External inaccessibility is more established than deletion, timing motive, or content relevance.
ingestion_conditional: The database's missing contents directly bound the assessable source universe.
framing_conditional: The same timeline can be framed as suspicious concealment or an ordinary outage later prolonged for security and political reasons.

notes: Track access state, content survival, motive, and origin relevance as separate claims.

framing_assumptions: Wuhan is selected after the event as a city unusually associated with bat-coronavirus research, making the co-location appear diagnostically rare.
policy_sensitivities: Weight depends on the reference class of cities, labs, outbreak-detection hubs, wildlife trade routes, and whether location hypotheses were specified in advance.
coverage_gaps: No quantified comparison estimates the probability of Wuhan under laboratory and natural-trade pathways using symmetric geographic models.
correlation_risks: Correlated with WIV capability and biosafety evidence, while competing market geography is represented by several overlapping items.

evidential: The co-location is real; its likelihood ratio is not established.
framing_conditional: Choosing the lab, market, city, or regional trade network as the salient geographic unit changes the apparent coincidence.

notes: A clear example of reference-class and salience choices entering before assessment.
